title: Isaac Sim motor-command stream -- PROVISIONAL
description: The control<->sim boundary (CONVENTIONS.md 'Trajectory / motor-command interface'): a time-stamped stream of four per-rotor angular-velocity setpoints (rad/s). Motor/propeller physics is Isaac Sim's job; the controller only emits the rad/s setpoint per rotor. PROVISIONAL -- 성진 waits on 윤호 to finalise the Isaac Sim JSON schema. NOTE ON TIME: 'time' is FLOAT SECONDS (성진 control clock), NOT integer nanoseconds (that is the separate vision/VIO stream, CONVENTIONS.md 'Timestamps'). The motor 'fps' is only nominal -- it is VARIABLE, so the per-frame 'time' is authoritative.

fps.description: Nominal command rate in frames per second (> 0). The motor command rate is VARIABLE; the authoritative time base is each frame's float-seconds 'time', not this fps.

frames.description: Ordered stream of motor-command frames (may be empty).

time.description: Timestamp in FLOAT SECONDS on 성진's control clock (e.g. 0.01), >= 0. Per-frame time is authoritative because the motor command rate is variable. NOT integer nanoseconds (that clock is the separate vision/VIO stream).

motor_cmd_w.description: Exactly four rotor angular-velocity setpoints [w1, w2, w3, w4] in rad/s. Rotor index->geometry and CW/CCW spin sign are fixed by Isaac Sim's rotor config (a 윤호 decision -- see interface/README.md), not by this schema.
